text:
Morel Mackernasey (モラウ＝マッカーナーシ, Morau Makkānāshi) is a Triple-Star Sea Hunter who uses a giant smoking pipe and smoke as weapons, and was one of the members of the Chimera Ant Extermination Team. He is also the master of both Knuckle Bine and Shoot McMahon.

Appearance

Morel is a tall, well-built middle-aged man with shoulder-length white hair and prominent facial features, including a broad face, a strong square chin, and a crooked nose. He is always seen wearing pince-nez sunglasses. He typically wears a red tie and a gray shirt, which he tucks into his pants. He also carries a giant smoking pipe.

Personality

Like his colleagues, Morel is very experienced in his field and demonstrates calm and patience. He is willing to wait for quite some time for his plans to come to fruition and is also able to keep his cool in the middle of battle. He is very perceptive of his opponents, particularly Cheetu and Leol, and uses his calm disposition to outwit both of them. As the teacher of both Knuckle and Shoot, Morel proves to have good leadership skills and coordination with his teammates, although, like most Manipulators, he also works accordingly by himself.

Despite all these, Morel is very emotional and has a great sense of compassion; he once cried during the exchange between Colt and the Chimera Ant Queen and promised Colt that he would protect him and "Reina" from harm. His emotional nature has rubbed off on his disciples, especially Knuckle Bine. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 215 

Background

After passing the Hunter Exam and learning Nen, Morel became Knuckle and Shoot's teacher. He and Knov are good comrades and both of them are members of the pro-Netero faction in the Hunter Association. At some point, Morel befriended Grachan and Likke.

Plot

Chimera Ant arc

The Ant Extermination Team arrive

When the V5 hire Chairman Netero to exterminate the Chimera Ants with any means, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 288 the Chairman enlists Morel and Knov's assistance, despite interference from Pariston. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 234 They are tasked with killing the Queen before she gives birth to the King, which is estimated to happen in two months at the earliest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 201 As the three accompany Netero to NGL, Knov and Morel leave their disciples in a town near the border. 

Morel makes his first appearance when he is about to cross into NGL with Netero and Knov. Upon meeting Killua at a checkpoint, he taunts the boy about the state of fear that he is in. Killua replies that he thinks the three of them won't stand much chance against Neferpitou, but Morel laughs it off, telling Killua that the outcome of a Nen battle is never certain and one has to fight with absolute confidence in victory. Upon hearing that Gon attempted to attack his enemy, he comments he has more of a future than Killua. The three then advance into NGL. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 19, Chapter 199 

When they come within sight of the nest, Netero wonders who between himself and Neferpitou is stronger. Morel expects him to fight the Chimera Ant anyway. Netero responds that even though their only target is the Queen, Neferpitou's En range makes a surgical attack on her impossible. They agree to whittle down the enemy's numbers slowly. Knov retrieves Morel's pipe from his pocket dimension. Claiming they can take it slow and enjoy it, the Sea Hunter creates an army of smoke bunnies to find out how many enemies they will have to deal with. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 202 Over the course of ten days, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 203 the trio manages to eliminate two squadrons, always starting from the leader. Netero reckons the Ants will use baits and traps, which Morel claims he and Knov can easily separate, but the latter suggests intentionally falling in traps at times, both to exterminate some of the more organized troops before they can all band together and to create chaos. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 204 

For their next plan, Morel creates a thick fog that covers an entire forest near the Ant Queen's colony while Knov places portals all over the ground. When a Chimera Ant falls into one, they find themselves in a large room conjured by Knov, where the Chairman kills them. Using this method, they manage to decimate 14 more squadrons. Morel feels their plan is unfair, to which Knov replies it is foolproof since, despite his modesty, Netero is still stronger than them and eager to fight. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 205 The Ants eventually wise up, and by the end of the fourth week, they hole up inside the nest. Knov contacts Netero and asks him if they should take action. The Chairman insists on waiting for their disciples. When Morel objects they would not be not useful in a fight of that magnitude, Knov comments Netero likes to gamble, and the two make a bet on whose pupils will show up the following day. Morel wagers 100,000 on Knuckle and Shoot, adding the clause that if they both lose, the pot goes to the Chairman. Knov bets for 1 million Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 206 that Gon, Killua, and Palm will come along too, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 214 which Morel regards as throwing money down the drain. 

The following day, they see Colt flying towards them. Knov opens a portal, but Morel notices the Ant is carrying a white flag. He comes to them with the intention of surrendering, telling them about the premature birth of the King and begging them to save the Queen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 213 He warns them that should the Queen die, the Squadron Leaders will leave the nest and try to spread their seed. Knov confirms to Morel it is possible. Morel accepts to take Colt to the Chairman but adds that he cannot promise Netero will want to help him or spare his life. When he accepts anyway, they enter Knov's portal. The four relocate to a sumptuous room where Netero requests the Hunter Association to send Dr. Lee, her best surgeons and artificial organ specialists to the Queen's nest. Knov and Morel are worried to see him don his "heart" t-shirts, which he reserves for serious fights. The Sea Hunter is amazed by Netero's polished aura flow, which gives him the impression needles are stabbing him. To his and Knov's astonishment, Colt estimates that the Chairman will be killed by one of the Royal Guards before he can reach the King. Netero leaves Morel in charge and prepares to leave, causing the Sea Hunter to worry that he is going to take on the King by himself. 

They return to the colony with the surgeons. Despite the doctors' efforts, all they can do is to prolong the dying moment of the Queen for a few minutes. Morel is shocked to discover some Ants retain memories from their lives as humans. When Colt finds a baby in the Queen's body, Morel admonishes him to leave and hide where he cannot find him if he cannot swear he and the baby will never eat a human; crying, he also vows that, if they can, he will protect them with his life. 

After Shoot and Knuckle capture Kite in the Queen's nest, Morel and the latter go to the Republic of Rokario to handle Cheetu, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 220 a former Squadron Leader with superhuman speed who killed and injured a number of humans in the vicinity of Pata City. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 218 The two of them ambush Cheetu on a road, with Morel hiding in a smokescreen and Knuckle standing in front of it. Cheetu effortlessly dodges Knuckle's punch and Morel's sneak attack, causing the Sea Hunter to praise his reflexes, and showers the two with punches without them being able to react. The two Hunters manage to follow him with their sight after a while, but their bodies are too slow to keep up with him. Morel blows more smoke, enveloping the three of them. When Knuckle moves in to attack Cheetu, Morel sends several smoke puppets with his appearance to encircle the Chimera Ant. Frozen by the surprise, Cheetu cannot prevent Knuckle from putting an A.P.R. on him. The former Squadron Leader tries to chase Knuckle in the smoke, but Morel trips him before retreating. The two Hunters hide in the surrounding vegetation with Zetsu. Cheetu runs away. Fearing he will get stronger, Morel contacts the Hunter Association to send someone who can match up well with him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 221 

Through Colt, the Extermination Team becomes aware of the "Sorting" and that the Royal Guards know how to awaken Nen by force. Netero infiltrates the Republic of East Gorteau and warns the Extermination Team to assume the worst if they do not hear from him by the appointed day. Ten days before the rally announced by Ming Jol-ik takes place, Knov, Morel, Knuckle, Shoot, Gon, and Killua take a train to East Gorteau. Morel remarks taking all of them breaks their promise to Netero, but Knov points out their agreement was for NGL, not East Gorteau, which Morel sees as splitting hairs. Nonetheless, he does not want to make them turn back. When Shoot voices his doubts about Gon, Morel replies he has the impression Gon has been saving his motivation to unleash it on his enemy. While they eat in a restaurant, Knov and Morel update the others on the "Sorting" and Netero's movements. At that moment, Netero sends Knov a text, causing Morel to wonder aloud if he has satellite dishes for ears, a comment that Netero anticipated. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 21, Chapter 223 

That night, Morel confronts Gon, pretending to have doubts about him being part of the Extermination Team. He orders him to strike him with his full power like Neferpitou. He removes his shirt and bulks up in preparation for Gon's attack, only to shiver and break into a cold sweat upon sensing his bloodlust and power. Killua stops Gon and asks Morel if it is enough. Morel laughs and approves of Gon, believing him to have the right touch of lunacy. Following the Chairman's instructions, the Extermination Team divides into three groups of two—Morel and Knov versus Shaiapouf, Knuckle and Shoot versus Menthuthuyoupi, Gon and Killua versus Neferpitou—and separate the Royal Guards from the King. 

Nine days prior to the planned attack, in the evening, Knov and Morel talk to Marcos, a military leader in Ming Jol-ik's government, persuading him to sign an agreement whereby he shall disclose all military information in exchange for asylum for himself and his family. Morel thanks him for his assistance, while condemning his hypocrisy to himself. He swallows his irritation and instructs Marcos to go about his daily routine as usual. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 22, Chapter 231 The two Hunters learn through Gon or Killua that the "Sorting" has already begun, and through Knuckle that Cheetu has escaped capture. Morel hangs up, on his pupil and contacts the Hunter Association, throwing a tantrum because the Review Board did not select Likke as he recommended. Morel and Knov discuss the corruption in the Hunter Association, with Morel growing so frustrated at the shady moves of the pro-Vice-Chairman faction to sabotage Chairman Netero's plan and win the next Chairman Election that he knocks Knov's book off his hand. He laments that letting 5,000,000 die to ensure the death of the King is too high a price. He receives a call from Marcos, who tells him that unrest is spreading through the country. When Ming, manipulated by the Chimera Ants, appears on television and announces the enforcing of martial law, Morel, rejoicing, realizes that Gon and Killua are responsible for obstructing the "Sorting". 

Morel puts the capital city Peijin under siege using his "Smoke Troopers", with Knov's assistance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 243 As he anticipated, Neferpitou recalls all their controlled puppets to protect the city, although the unpredictable behavior of the King is a minor source of worry for Morel. Three days later, Morel runs into Cheetu again, noticing he has become stronger and that A.P.R. is gone, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 244 which leads him to infer the Chimera Ants have an Exorcist on their side. He resolves to fight Cheetu in case he is the one. He activates Deep Purple, but Cheetu runs through the smoke and punches him. He initially assumes that the Ant has not changed his tactics. To his shock, the two are teleported to a savanna-like space. Cheetu excitedly announces they are starting a game of tag and that Morel has 8 hours to tag him. Morel, however, begins exploring his surroundings. Fearing the aura from Deep Purple would not return to him if he deactivated his "Smoke Troopers", he decides to make do with his remaining aura. He tries smashing the hourglass, to no avail. He asks Cheetu what will happen if he does not catch him in time, noticing his ability was influenced by Hakoware, but the Ant does not reply. Morel thus lays down and declares he is going to sleep for five hours while thinking that Cheetu does not understand that his own ability is not a contest of speed, but of wills. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 245 

Morel keeps pretending to try to sleep while Cheetu pesters him. Annoyed, he swings his pipe at him and lies back down, thinking that as soon as the Ant grows tired and takes his eyes off him, it is his victory. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 246 When Cheetu starts to read, Morel creates a rope out of some smoke and ties it around his ankle. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 23, Chapter 247 He then makes a clone of himself, to whom he leaves his pipe with the rope, with his smoke and covers himself with grass-colored smoke. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 248 Around the four-hour mark, he makes the clone stand up and point out the rope to Cheetu, who unsuccessfully tries to run away and break it while the clone reels him in. Cornered, Cheetu conjures a crossbow and claws. The clone easily blocks his arrow and provokes Cheetu by insulting his intelligence. Enraged, Cheetu stabs it with the claws, only to realize it was made out of smoke. Morel then touches his shoulder, stating he should have never looked away from him. The two are teleported back to Peijin, with Cheetu losing the will to fight. Morel enquires what would have happened had he failed to catch him. Cheetu replies it does not matter, as he can no longer use that ability. In high spirits, he rushes off to get another ability from Shaiapouf, with Morel wishing there were more enemies that easy to deal with. 

Morel surmises one of the Royal Guards has the power to bestow Nen abilities onto others. He is contacted by Knov, who takes him in his pocket dimension and informs him that two more Ants, a lobster and a wolf, are looking for them. He adds that there seems to be a stalemate, which he attributes to his taking out Flutter. Later that same day, all of Neferpitou's puppets, as well as their En, disappear, which causes the two Hunters to realize something serious has happened in the Royal Palace. Morel advances that it could be a trap, but Knov states the Royal Guards would never do anything that could endanger the King. The two split up—Knov is going to infiltrate the palace to create an "exit" while Morel stays in Peijin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 249 

Morel notices he is being followed by Leol and, due to his lack of knowledge about the area, is led in an underground chapel. Morel takes note of the Ant's confidence, which he believes will make him careless. He waits excitedly while Leol prepares his ability. He is surprised to see the Ant wear earphones. When he mentions being a fan of the second album of the band Black Planet, Morel thinks it is difficult to fight someone with the same tastes as him. He is shocked when Leol activates Inamura, instantly recognizing it as Grachan's ability. He manages to deflect Leol's harpoon strike but is cut by his surfboard. He angrily claims the ability does not belong to Leol and swings his pipe at him. Dodging the attacks, Leol uses Tornave and sucks him into a whirlpool. Unable to move due to the pressure, he manages to free himself by exhaling. Forming another raft with his smoke to stand atop the water, he claims his turn has come. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 253 

He tells Leol he has realized three things about his ability, starting by saying he can borrow other people's powers. By stating his earphones are only for show, he lures Leol into talking back, confirming he is not restricting one of his senses to strengthen his ability. He concludes the list by angrily claiming Leol stole Inamura from his friend Grachan and vows to kill him. He dismisses Leol's defense that he is only borrowing the ability. When the Ant raises a tsunami with Big Wall, Morel takes a deep breath and lets himself be submerged again. Sitting at the bottom of the chapel, he forms his smoke into hoses and begins to exhale, rapidly converting the oxygen in the closed locale into CO 2 , poisoning Leol and causing him to drown. Morel then swims out of the chapel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 254 

Upon reuniting with Knov, Morel discovers his friend has had a mental breakdown upon seeing Shaiapouf's aura, although he successfully planted all the portals. Two days before the raid, he updates the other Extermination Team members on the situation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 255 On the eve of the attack, Killua, Gon, and Ikalgo contact him to tell him they suspect Neferpitou rescinded their En to treat the King. Morel objects that the Royal Guards would have never injured him and that all other Ants would have been unable to. Their hypothesis that the King hurt himself leaves him feeling flabbergasted and uneasy. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 258 

At 2 p.m., 10 hours before they strike, Morel, Shoot, Knuckle, and Meleoron gather in Knov's pocket dimension. When Knov offers to keep watch over the Royal Guards, Morel warns him to watch out for Neferpitou's irregularly-shaped En. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 259 Morel welcomes the news that Shaiapouf is hypnotizing the people gathered for the "Sorting" with relief, as that will prevent mass panic. At 6 p.m., Gon, Killua, and Ikalgo enter the pocket dimension too. Gon asks if they have heard from Palm, which they haven't. Morel states they will start moving at 11:30. He notes with regret that the continuous battles have left him at 35% of his power, but with Knov's withdrawal from the mission, he intends to go on. When the time comes, he gives the signal so the Extermination Team can move to a room whose exit is closer to the palace. They wait outside for 20 minutes, keeping an eye on the palace. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 24, Chapter 260 They all agree to infiltrate through the portal closest to the throne room, the one at the foot of the central stairway. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 261 

The Extermination Team enters the last room seven minutes before the attack, with Morel stating that they will gather around the exit in six minutes. He overhears Killua and Ikalgo talk about unforeseen circumstances and, barging in, suggests one of them would be a woman since one of the King's goals is to reproduce and his activities in the palace are unknown. Knuckle advances that Bizeff may have been procuring women not for himself, but for the King, which leads the Extermination Team, with the exception of Gon, to worry about Palm's condition. Morel declares no amends will be made to the plan regardless of her state. Killua is still concerned about the King hurting himself and Morel proposes a brainstorming of possible scenarios. Gon states that the inability to forgive oneself is one. From it, Morel deduces that the King is proud and would never do anything against his will, which makes combat unavoidable. He then tells the others to gather in front of the exit. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 262 Ten seconds before midnight, he begins the countdown. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 263 He is the last one to storm the palace. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 265 

The Palace Invasion

As the Extermination Team emerge at the central stairway, they are surprised to find Menthuthuyoupi present instead of Neferpitou's En. Menthuthuyoupi quickly transforms to engage the team in combat, but both parties are caught off guard by Zeno's Dragon Dive raining down on the palace. A shot falls right in front of Morel, which he barely manages to avoid by quickly moving back. Despite momentarily freezing in surprise at Dragon Dive, Morel begins to release smoke. He and the rest of the team see Gon continue charging straight at Menthuthuyoupi, and are momentarily confused at why Gon changed, but can only be amazed when they, less than a second later, realize how quickly he considered the possibility that Dragon Dive had quietly taken out the team member assigned to engage Menthuthuyoupi. Shoot shivers and freezes at Gon's instinctiveness, and Morel wordlessly wills Shoot to not blame himself and to regain his composure to handle Neferpitou now that Gon and Killua had engaged Menthuthuyoupi; even he had been slower than Killua to realize Gon's intent. Shoot calms down and thinks of the advice from his friends and mentors, and engages Menthuthuyoupi with a newfound confidence. Morel sees Youpi regain his confidence, and is relieved. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 266 

Before Gon finishes closing the gap between him and Menthuthuyoupi, the Extermination Team see Menthuthuyoupi get hit in the side, and smile at the knowledge that Knuckle and Meleoron had survived Dragon Dive. After Knuckle successfully activates Hakoware, a shiver runs down his spine at his estimate that Menthuthuyoupi's bottomless aura reserves could be more than ten times Morel's own reserves. Neferpitou's En suddenly floods the staircase and envelopes the Extermination Team, causing them to stiffen and giving Menthuthuyoupi an opening, which he uses to destroy the central staircase with a swift attack. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 267 

Morel finds himself at the bottom of the stairs. He calls out to Shoot to entrust him with a matter, before taking advantage of the dust cloud to use his Deep Purple to provoke Menthuthuyoupi into giving an opening. He has a clone take his pipe and swing at Menthuthuyoupi to create a second diversion, allowing him to successfully slip by and proceed up the destroyed stairs, but leaving him with no alternative but to run. Menthuthuyoupi sees that the pipe was carried by a clone and sees Morel running away, but a second punch from an invisible Knuckle prevents him from stopping Morel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 268 Morel's two disciples continue chipping at Menthuthuyoupi, and Shoot finds the chance in the middle of a barrage of attacks launched at Menthuthuyoupi to have one of his Disembodied Hands grab Morel's pipe and return it to him. Morel gives his disciples a thumbs up and thinks about drinking themselves silly after their situation is over. The dust created helps Morel diffuse his smoky aura throughout the palace and identify that his target, Shaiapouf, is in the throne room. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 269 

Fighting Shaiapouf

Before nine seconds even pass from the beginning of the invasion, Morel steps into the throne room, and engages Shaiapouf. He releases his smoky aura, then calls out to Shaiapouf, but is annoyed when Shaiapouf does not even respond to him and tries to fly off. Morel guesses that Shaiapouf is trying to fly to the King, but sees Shaiapouf collide with his Smoky Jail, and tells him that he had him locked in. Shaiapouf wipes his tears and asks Morel what he could do to get Morel to let him out, but Morel apologizes and tells him that he could not do so. Shaiapouf accepts this, and begins to scatter his Spiritual Message. Elsewhere, as Gon and Killua run around to find their opponent, Gon recognizes Morel's Smoky Jail surrounding the throne room, and uses it to deduce that his opponent was somewhere else. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 25, Chapter 270 

Shaiapouf asks Morel if the act of confining him was the right choice, and says that he would not mind finding out. Morel does not respond, but keeps in mind that Knov had said Shaiapouf's butterfly dust had a hypnotic effect, and makes a mental note not to breathe it in. He remains posed, ready for combat. Shaiapouf compliments his choice to stop talking, and senses Morel's emotions of vigilance, resolve, and confidence through his Spiritual Message. After analyzing Morel's emotions, Shaiapouf decides to introduce himself to Morel, only to find Morel continuing to not respond, and becoming more vigilant. He tells Morel that he felt hurt by Morel, and rapidly folds his wings around himself to form a chrysalis. Morel notes Shaiapouf's transformation, and finds satisfaction in his choice to take his time, regardless of what he would turn into. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 26, Chapter 273 

Roughly 23 seconds after the Extermination Team first invaded the castle, Meleoron finds himself looking for allies to assist. He sees Morel's Smoky Jail and decides to look for Gon and Killua. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 26, Chapter 276 After injuring Shoot and making Knuckle flee, Menthuthuyoupi calls out to the other Royal Guards, and wonders where they are. Knuckle retrieves Shoot and sees Menthuthuyoupi searching for his companions, and has a brief moment of arrogance from the fact that Menthuthuyoupi was guarding the empty throne room, stemming from his knowledge that Morel was using his Smoky Jail in the throne room. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 26, Chapter 277 

Roughly two minutes and forty seconds after the beginning of the invasion, Menthuthuyoupi comes across Morel's Smoky Jail and begins fruitlessly attacking it, calling out to the King and his companions at the same time. Morel hears Menthuthuyoupi and wonders if Knuckle and Shoot had been killed, and hopes that Netero is doing well. He wonders why Shaiapouf was not answering Menthuthuyoupi, and wonders why Shaiapouf was staying in his cocoon and not rushing to the King's side. Morel begins to wonder if they had miscalculated something about the Royal Guards, and has conflicting thoughts about whether the plan was progressing correctly or not. From inside his cocoon, Shaiapouf continues to sense Morel's emotions, and sees that despite the addition of negative emotions like suspicion, anxiety, and impatience, Morel's conviction remained unchanged, the emotional profile of an admirable warrior. He makes plans to take advantage of Morel's emotions, expecting them to inhibit his decision making. 

As Zeno walks away from the palace, Cheetu comes across him, and tries to goad him into a fight. Cheetu says that he would rather fight Morel, but was unable to because Morel had locked himself inside his smoke screen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 26, Chapter 278 

Narration describes how Morel's Smoky Jail protected the third floor of the central tower from most of Menthuthuyoupi's explosive attacks. In the throne room, Morel tries to reassure himself that keeping Shaiapouf locked up with him no matter what was going on outside should be the correct decision. Narration reveals that, roughly 3 minutes and 20 seconds after the palace invasion began, unbeknownst to Morel, about 100 trillion microscopic bugs, small enough to slip between smoke particles in intervals imperceptible to Morel were slowly but surely escaping his Smoky Jail. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 26, Chapter 279 

Ikalgo, while possessing Flutter's corpse, runs towards a group of warehouses in the basement north of the palace, to try and locate Palm. While exploring the warehouses, he comes across a note secretly written with aura, only readable with Gyo, from Palm telling its reader to assume that she was dead. Ikalgo remembers Morel's instructions, to use the satellite dragonflies and his telepathy to provide backup to the ants, by pretending to give status reports to his superior but informing Meleoron instead, confusing the enemies with a mixture of truth and lies. 

Eventually, the thought that Shaiapouf did not answer Menthuthuyoupi because he could not answer crosses Morel's mind. Because of his assessment that Shaiapouf should currently be a defenseless, shapeless mass transforming inside the pupa, Morel feels a desire to attack. He holds back, continuing to observe the pupa, and wonders if Shaiapouf would really make such a fatal error in their current situation. Morel decides that Shaiapouf's cocoon is definitely a trap, but anguishes over whether the trap would be triggered by his attack, or if the goal was to waste his time. While he tries to figure out what the right decision is, Shaiapouf observes Morel hesitate because of how excellent he is as a soldier as he completes his reconstitution outside Morel's Smoky Jail Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 281 Morel takes a half turn backwards and raises his pipe. Shaiapouf wonders if Morel will be able to abandon his original plan to stall him without evidence. Right as Shaiapouf concludes that Morel is unable to do so, Morel swings at Shaiapouf's cocoon, and deactivates his Smoky Jail, and sees Shaiapouf standing outside the previously enclosed area. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 283 

As Shaiapouf mentally congratulates Morel on being able to make a decision, Morel explains that the decision was simple, since Shaiapouf's pupa appeared after he learnt of Morels plans, and so had to be a trap. Morel adds that Shaiapouf's lack of reaction as he prepared to strike gave him more confidence that he was not inside the pupa. Shaiapouf chuckles at Morel's analysis, then tells him that fifteen minutes was his original estimate of how long Morel would waste before ultimately deciding to deactivate his smoke and act. Morel retorts by saying that Shaiapouf had a low opinion of him, if he thought that Morel would stand in front of an empty shell for fifteen minutes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 284 

Shaiapouf says that he had given Morel too much credit, before jumping high into the air and splitting himself into fragments with his Beelzebub, revealing how he slipped through Morel's Smoky Jail without being seen. Shaiapouf's fragments taunt Morel, thanking him for releasing him, and Morel activates his Deep Purple soldiers to swat at Shaiapouf's fragments. Morel observes the destroyed fragments reassemble, and can only watch helplessly as the swarm of insects flies away. He reassures himself that attacking the pupa was his best option at the time, and reminds himself that dwelling on the past was playing into Shaiapouf's hand, and turns his focus to what to do then and anticipating Shaiapouf's next moves of regrouping with the other Royal Guards and the King. Shaiapouf materializes on Morel's back and grabs his pipe, then separates him from his weapon by kicking Morel to the ground. Shaiapouf quickly flies away before Morel can get up, and despite Morel's Deep Purple soldiers ability to leap much higher into the sky than the average human, Shaiapouf escapes with Morel's weapon. 

Fighting Menthuthuyoupi

Morel quickly assesses his defeat and concludes that he cannot deactivate Deep Purple. He senses a strong presence behind him, and turns to find Menthuthuyoupi staring at him in quiet anger. Menthuthuyoupi asks Morel if he is the one that can teleport, but before he can respond, Knuckle appears a distance behind Menthuthuyoupi and makes his presence known. Morel acts quickly and transforms his Deep Purple soldiers into 89 copies of Knuckle, trying to help Knuckle act. Menthuthuyoupi transforms, excited at the chance to attack. Menthuthuyoupi swipes at Morel's many clones and whittles down at their numbers. After some time passes without Knuckle attacking, Menthuthuyoupi intentionally leaves an opening, but is enraged when he finds that a clone had taken the bait, and turns to Morel, cursing at him in anger. Morel tells Menthuthuyoupi that the real Knuckle was never there, causing a rage-swollen Menthuthuyoupi to lunge at Morel to kill him. Roughly seven and a half minutes after the invasion begins, as Morel turns and runs, Knuckle leaps back in and lands a solid punch on Menthuthuyoupi, making him explode in rage. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 285 

Shaiapouf flies to a river outside the palace and throws Morel's pipe into the river. Satisfied that he had eliminated the threat of the "smoke man", Shaiapouf re-fragments himself using Beelzebub and makes his way back to the palace. He happily thinks about how he would have been unable to escape if Morel had not released him, cutting his combat potential in half. Shaiapouf thinks about how Morel should have deliberated more carefully, asking whether the pupa's contents had escaped the smoke when he first realized it was empty, and asking whether a voice appearing to speak from outside was Shaiapouf's real voice or not. He thinks about how the "plan" he had "divulged" to Morel was in essence a suicide mission, and if he had not released Shaiapouf, his comrades could likely have eventually identified the ones they fought in combat as fakes, making it easy to conclude that the real one was still trapped and easily caught. Shaiapouf shows his respect for Morel's formidability by admitting to being roughly 10% resigned to being killed by Morel in that manner, and concludes that the explosions he heard outside must have shaken Morel's resolve, before speeding off toward the palace. Narration reveals that Morel had been in bad shape even before the palace invasion began, and that as a result of facing Shaiapouf in a mental battle under tremendous stress, jumped the gun due to fatigue and anxiety. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 286 

Through his clones, Shaiapouf observes the battles going on, and sees Menthuthuyoupi in combat with the "smoke man". He guesses that only one of the "punks" is real, and that the rest were smoke, unable to be replaced with the pipe he had just gotten rid off. Narration reveals that Morel's choice to intervene then was the only moment he could have done so to prevent a one-on-one duel between Menthuthuyoupi and Knuckle, and so the timing in which his smoky jail was released was a miraculous feat, not a matter from luck, but the effect of Morel's vast experience; Morel's decision that his initial judgment of how his team matched up against the enemy was too optimistic led him to choose to avoid the emotional turmoil of whether to support his allies or maintain his entrapment of Shaiapouf by simply attacking the pupa, saving Knuckle in the process. 

As Knuckle leaps away from Menthuthuyoupi's explosion, he thanks Morel for the lesson he had drilled into him in the past, to notice when the doubles of him had a particularly angry expression and take that as a signal to pause and refocus on his goal. Despite Menthuthuyoupi's aura feeling inexhaustible, Morel continues to distract and engage Menthuthuyoupi, making him more and more angry with each clone that successfully tricked him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 287 After going down to ten clones remaining, Morel begins to assume that Menthuthuyoupi had already noticed him and was saving him for last, and decides to struggle as long as he could. He places all his soldiers without Menthuthuyoupi's sight to better keep track of him, but also because Morel was too worn out to disperse them over a wider area, risking the soldiers vanishing if Morel stopped concentrating. As Morel runs out of energy and willpower, he convinces himself that the battle would be over soon, and that Menthuthuyoupi could not have infinite aura reserves, but the point where Morel could no longer stand came faster than Menthuthuyoupi's aura bankruptcy. 

Roughly nine minutes and thirty seconds after the palace invasion's beginning, Morel looks at the ground, without even enough energy to look up. Menthuthuyoupi defeats Morel's final clone and leaps over to him, and lets him know that he had seen Morel midway through but wanted to save him for the last. Morel is unable to respond. Menthuthuyoupi sees this and dismisses his existence, having displayed astonishing growth during combat, far beyond Morel and the others had grown. Still, Menthuthuyoupi tells Morel how he and his team were amazing. Perhaps because these words were so out of character for Menthuthuyoupi, Morel accepts them as his true feelings. Menthuthuyoupi tells Morel that because he was so amazing, he had to kill him properly. Morel accepts his defeat, and in that moment, admits that he did not mind being killed by Menthuthuyoupi. 

Menthuthuyoupi swings. He looks at where he struck and sees nothing. He looks at his blade and sees blood, and concludes that Morel had turned invisible, with the assistance of a friend who had the ability to turn others invisible as well. He sees the blood trail and starts following it. Meleoron, who had been helping Morel stagger away, anguishes at potentially being forced to abandon Morel, but Knuckle makes himself known, and draws Menthuthuyoupi's attention away. He tells Menthuthuyoupi to let them go in exchange for taking him on, but Menthuthuyoupi declines Knuckle's offer, and instead demands him release his Hakoware instead. Morel calls out and shouts at Knuckle not to, but his cries remain unheard due to Meleoron's God's Accomplice. Meleoron abandons Morel so that Knuckle can hear his cries, but the sight of a bloodied Morel struggling on the ground with Menthuthuyoupi walking towards him is enough for Knuckle to disperse his Hakoware, eleven minutes and forty seconds after the beginning of the palace invasion, even through Morel's cries for him not to. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 289 

Morel and Meleoron collapse to the ground at losing their only way of defeating Menthuthuyoupi. Knuckle's will had crumbled when images of Shoot flashed before his eyes after seeing Morel covered in blood. Menthuthuyoupi prepares to leave, and ignores Knuckles attempts to get him to fight. Knuckle calls out to try and get Menthuthuyoupi to stop, but Menthuthuyoupi simply tells them to rush Morel to a doctor, otherwise their trade would have been for nothing. Meleoron calls out to a dazed Knuckle to get assistance in helping him carry Morel, but Morel swats him away as soon as Knuckle tries to help him up. He tells the two to leave him and go and continue their plan, encouraging Knuckle to chase down Menthuthuyoupi and immediately fight him again. Knuckle confesses his newfound inability to do so, stemming from him abandoning his recognition of Menthuthuyoupi as the enemy. Knov appears and tells Knuckle to leave Morel with him, telling him that he had also rescued Shoot, and that Netero had taking the King to the designated spot as planned. As he leaves, he gives Knuckle a final chance to leave, and compliments Knuckle's resilience after hearing his choice to stay. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 290 

After the battles

After the Chimera Ant Extermination mission is completed, Morel is hospitalized. He makes his report to Beans on the phone, stating that Netero succeeded but that he could not verify Meruem's death in person, a matter he entrusted to Palm. At Beans' objection, Morel spitefully retorts that those who are out on the field trust the competence of their comrades. The secretary warns him the Vice Chairman's faction will not accept that logic and that he might be subjected to a hearing if the information leaks. Morel then contacts Colt, informing him that the surviving Chimera Ants will be classified as Magical Beasts and that their role in the incident in East Gorteau was covered up. He overhears the child Colt has found in the Queen's remains protest that her name is not Reina, but Kite. Morel rushes to tell Gon and Killua. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 30, Chapter 316 

13 th Hunter Chairman Election arc

Morel has the only official Exorcist in the Hunter Association come to the hospital to lift the Vow that put Gon in a coma and risks killing him, but she claims there is nothing she can do. Leorio visits Gon and has a heated conversation with Killua and his butler Gotoh on the phone. He refuses to hand his phone over to Morel, who calmly tells him to do so when he hears a demand that to him sounds impossible. Gotoh says to heal Gon, there must be nobody but Gon in the building, to which Leorio replies they cannot evacuate a national hospital. Morel takes the phone and assures Gotoh that his condition will be met by building a wing just for Gon in the parking lot. He then calls Knov, telling him to prepare automatic machinery and that the contents of the room should be invisible to all. He agrees it sounds like Killua is bringing an Exorcist, but from the way the boy spoke he suspects it is something else. He insists to honor Killua's request and not make any other questions. Hearing this, Leorio leaves him his phone as he heads out to have a discussion with someone. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 325 

Soon afterward, he finishes 8 th in the fourth round of the 13th Hunter Chairman Election and becomes one of the 16 candidates in the fifth round. It is also suggested that he may qualify for a Triple-Star Hunter License thanks to his contributions to the extermination of the Chimera Ants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 326 

Killua calls him to explain that his brother Illumi is chasing him down to kill Alluka, the only person who, through granting wishes, is capable of healing Gon, and that he will manipulate civilians to do so. Killua asks him to inform Teradein's faction of his brother's heinous actions so they will hunt him down to further their own agenda. Morel agrees with the stipulation that he'll tell them Illumi himself is too dangerous to pursue. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 328 Morel passes the fifth round of the election, but is pushed off the list of candidates in the sixth round. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 329 

Teradein asks Morel to become their new captain after Bushidora's death. Undeterred, Teradein announces in a video to the Hunters that negotiations with Morel are underway, which prompts Morel to respond in kind. In his video, he stresses he has no intention of being part of their faction or becoming Chairman, and criticizes him for ignoring his advice and dispersing his forces, also warning him that threatening to leak the killers' information will get them killed—which, in fact, it does. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 330 

When Killua and Alluka enter the special wing where Gon is and Nanika begins to heal him, Morel is appalled by the monstrous aura he perceives. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 333 After Gon is fully healed he leads him to Leorio, who is in the voting room where all the Hunters are voting for the 13 th chairman, giving him a thumbs-up while crying. While everybody celebrates Gon's recovery, Morel requests from Leorio not let Gon know that it was thanks to Killua, he was healed. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 334 Later, Knov hands Morel the money from their past bet since Morel's students were the ones who arrived on the agreed date. Morel uses the money they had both waged to buy an expensive bottle of wine and drink to Netero's memory, placing a glass in front of a picture of the late chairman. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 339 

Succession Contest arc

Ginta reveals Morel has agreed to take the navigation from the New Continent's border to the Gate upon himself, so he suggests leaving it in the hands of his team. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 346 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

Equipment

Pipe: Morel uses an oversized tobacco pipe as the medium for his Nen abilities and, should the need arise, as a melee weapon. It is composed of a white mouthpiece, a golden handle, and a shank, both adorned with swirling high reliefs, a straight, black stem, and a spherical bowl of dark beige in color. Morel carries the pipe on his shoulder. When not in use, he keeps it wrapped in a gray canvas. The Royal Guard Shaiapouf dumped it into a river during the palace invasion. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 27, Chapter 286 It is unknown if Morel retrieved or replaced it afterward.

Abilities & Powers

Morel has all the benefits granted by his status as a Hunter. He is both a good strategist and an experienced, resourceful fighter. He uses his pipe not only for his Nen abilities but also as a melee weapon, which he swings single-handedly and without effort despite its weight. He also possesses an unmatched lung capacity and a strong spirit. By his own claim, his abilities work better as support, and he doesn't think of himself as a very strong fighter; in spite of this, he strategically defeated two powerful Squadron Leaders. By increasing the size of his muscles, he can enhance his strength and defense in preparation for a strong attack. He displayed this ability when testing the power of Gon's Rock after the latter recovered his Nen. He is also valued by the Zodiacs for his navigation abilities, taking it upon himself to steer their ship in the waters of the Dark Continent. 

Preternatural Perception: Morel was able to tell that Cheetu had powered up with with only a glance. 

Enhanced Strength: Morel can wield his giant pipe as a club without effort, both with one and two hands and even lift it with just two fingers. His clone, whose power is allegedly much lower than Morel's, could single-handedly stop Cheetu in spite of the latter's leg strength. With a single swipe of his pipe, he was able to send one of Neferpitou's controlled humans flying into the distance. 

Enhanced Speed and Reflexes: After being repeatedly struck by Cheetu's fast punches, he was able to see Cheetu's movements and rapidly got accustomed to his speed. A clone of him managed to intercept an arrow shot by Cheetu at mid-range easily. 

Advanced Agility: Morel can run on the rooftops and jump from one to another while carrying his huge pipe on his back. 

Superhuman Lung Capacity: As a Sea Hunter, Morel has excellent lungs, allowing him to stay underwater for an extended period of time without taking any breath. He is confident that they are second to none. It is his proficiency in apnea that helped him win the battle against Leol in an underground church filled with water, first by letting him stay safe at the bottom, then by allowing him to exhale immense quantities of carbon dioxide to poison his opponent. Despite emitting enough of it to fill a good portion of a church, he managed to swim without breathing long enough to escape the maze-like building. 

Proficient Strategist: Morel can formulate accurate predictions on his opponent's move and has proved to be able to analyze hostile capabilities and thought patterns efficiently. He can devise effective strategies in a matter of instants, always exploiting his or his students' capabilities to the fullest. According to Shaiapouf, his determination, unshakable will, and confidence in himself bolster his defensive powers greatly. Also, Morel's years of experience can unconsciously lead him to pick the best option.

Weapon Specialist: Although he rarely switches to close-quarters combat, Morel can use his giant pipe as a club, delivering strong blows with only one hand. 

Nen

Morel is a Manipulator. He is a veteran hailed as a very capable Nen user, and his two pupils are capable fighters in their own right. Knuckle, who has fought more than 5000 battles, claims Morel has the most abundant aura pool he had ever seen prior to meeting Youpi, amounting to 70,000 aura units, more than three times larger than Gon's (21,500 aura units). Hunter × Hunter - Volume 20, Chapter 211 At 35% of his peak condition, Morel spread his smoky aura over a large area, summoned and controlled several smoke puppets, and maintained his impregnable Smoky Jail active for a few minutes. He is capable of using Ken, although he was frightened by an enraged Gon's Jajanken, and the latter indeed apologized, claiming his attack could have killed him. Morel is also a proficient Zetsu user, which, coupled with his ability to manipulate the smoke from his pipe, allows him to conceal his position to hide or launch a surprise attack. 

On top of Manipulation, Morel appears to be skilled in Enhancement, which he uses to increase his defensive abilities. Morel easily withstood Cheetu's numerous, rapid blows. Moreover, he successfully held his breath when hit by the waves and maelstrom produced by Leol, as well as enduring the relatively high water pressure seemingly without effort. Shaiapouf, one of the Royal Guards, did not do any noticeable damage to him with a stomping kick, after which Morel was quickly back on his feet. It also appears to grant him with exceptional recovery abilities, since he was able to stand up and run a mere few hours after suffering a deep, potentially lethal wound. 

Morel is also proficient at Emission and, possibly, Transmutation, despite the latter being the category he has the lowest affinity for. Since it is made of or imbued with aura, the smoke functions as an En of sorts, allowing him to sense the presence of anyone engulfed in it with enough accuracy to tell people apart. With this ability, he was able to cover at least a few floors of Ming Jol-ik's palace. This effectively prevents him from being obstructed by his own smoke screens. The usage of Transmutation or Conjuration might also be at the heart of his ability to change the color of his smoke; however, the consistency of the creation remains similar to that of smoke, so the trick becomes obvious upon contact. Despite being made of or containing aura, many Nen users have been tricked into mistaking it for "normal" smoke or dust. It can even be utilized underwater. The downside to Morel's smoke-related abilities is that he cannot activate new ones without his pipe and is forced to rely on existing smoky aura in that event.

Trivia

Morel is in possession of the best lung capacity in the whole series; ironically, he is also its grandest smoker.
However, despite his signature identity revolving much around the notion of smoking, it is unclear whether or not Morel is actually a smoker himself: the smoke in his ability Deep Purple is transmuted from his Nen, and he so far has never been seen lighting his giant pipe for smoke or smoking outside of combat.
The names of Morel's abilities, the preview for Episode 107, and the fact that the character is based on Ramo Nakajima make it possible that what Morel smokes may not be tobacco smoke.
Morel's eyes are never shown. He wears sunglasses even underwater and at night. He was also allowed to wear his sunglasses inside NGL, although his pipe was transported via Knov's Hide and Seek ability.
According to the "Hunter × Hunter Manual" section found in the Yu ☆ Yu ☆ Hakusho official character book (Shueisha Jump remix), Morel's stats are:

Intertextuality and References

His name in rōmaji, Morau Makkānāshi, is an anagram-like reference to Ramo Nakajima, a respected Japanese cult novel writer who often wore sunglasses.
The name Morel is similar to the character Morrel, a ship-owner from the classical novel The Count of Monte Cristo who meets the titular character in the first chapter. Morel himself is a Sea Hunter.
His trademark technique Deep Purple is a reference to an English rock band with the same name. Moreover, the kanji for this technique refers to the famous song "Purple Haze" by the musician Jimi Hendrix.
During his fight with Leol, Morel creates a raft out of smoke to stand atop the water. This might refer to the famous Deep Purple song "Smoke on the Water".
His technique Smoky Jail's alternate name, "Jailhouse Rock", is a reference to Elvis Presley's famous song.
Likewise, his disciple Knuckle wears his hair in a pompadour, a hairstyle commonly associated with the singer.
